mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate indicates a weak labor market and overall economic distress, leading to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investor confidence wanes.
evidence_refs: fact:High unemployment typically correlates with economic downturns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE is above 6.0% for three consecutive months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve begins to raise the federal funds rate significantly, it signals a tightening monetary policy, increasing the cost of capital and reducing future earnings valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25% in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Fed rate hikes historically precede market corrections.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a cumulative 0.25% FEDFUNDS hike over a three-month period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous acceleration in both retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based strength in consumer spending and investment, signaling robust economic growth that supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer and housing activity are key drivers of GDP.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods when both RSAFS and HOUST show year-over-year growth above their 5-year averages, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial recession, a continued decline in industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, which translates into negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downwards.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO is contracting year-over-year during an NBER-defined recession, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP signals an economic recession, leading to widespread concerns about corporate earnings and consumer demand, which typically results in significant negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in a downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP contraction is the definition of a recession.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods immediately following a reported quarterly contraction in GDPC1, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high consumer price inflation erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, while also increasing the likelihood of restrictive monetary policy, collectively leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can lead to higher interest rates.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 consistently produces positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.0% for three consecutive months, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in non-farm payrolls and a decrease in the unemployment rate indicates a robust labor market recovery, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong employment growth is a hallmark of economic recovery.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to deliver positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both PAYEMS shows positive monthly growth and UNRATE declines for three consecutive months, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a low interest rate environment, strong housing starts indicate robust consumer confidence and investment in real estate, which has positive spillover effects on related industries and contributes to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 shows negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is above its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.
